# Experiment Results: Catching and Reducing AI-Feel

*Generated by analyze.py*

## Data coverage

10 of the 12 planned models produced data. `x-ai/grok-4.3` and `mistralai/mistral-large-2411` returned HTTP 404 ("No endpoints available matching your guardrail restrictions") for every call on this OpenRouter account and are excluded. The deslop fixer (`anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6`) and the blind judge (`anthropic/claude-opus-4.6`, the highest-priority available judge candidate; `google/gemini-2.5-pro` was not on the account) are both outside the 12-model test set. 400 generations (100 per condition) and 300 valid judgements (0 invalid).

## 1. CATCH — Baseline AI-Feel by Model

Models ranked by mean plainly density score (higher = more AI tells).

## 2. IMPROVE — Intervention Effectiveness

Δdensity and Δcv are **paired** differences (intervention − baseline) per model×prompt. Negative Δdensity = fewer AI tells. Wilcoxon p < 0.05 indicates significant change.

| Condition | N pairs | Δdensity (mean) | p(Wilcoxon) | Δcv (mean) | p(Wilcoxon) | Judge win-rate | 95% CI |
|-----------|---------|----------------|-------------|-----------|-------------|--------------|--------|
| human_system | 100 | -0.540 | 0.000 | -0.007 | 0.734 | 0.93 (97 pairs) | [0.88, 0.98] |
| fewshot | 100 | -0.528 | 0.000 | 0.019 | 0.254 | 0.77 (97 pairs) | [0.69, 0.86] |
| deslop | 100 | -0.811 | 0.000 | 0.071 | 0.000 | 0.95 (96 pairs) | [0.90, 0.99] |

### PLAINLY vs Judge Divergence (Goodharting Check)

No divergence detected: PLAINLY and judge agree on direction for all interventions.

## 3. Limitations


- **LLM judge bias:** The judge model may have its own stylistic preferences that don't represent the full range of human readers. Its training data may overlap with or bias toward certain models.
- **Temperature variance:** Each text is generated at temperature 0.7, so repeated sampling would yield different outputs. The reported statistics reflect a single sample per model×prompt×condition.
- **Prompt set size:** 10 prompts is a small sample. Register effects and model rankings may not generalise.
- **OpenRouter model drift:** Model identifiers on OpenRouter may point to updated versions over time, meaning "GPT-4o" today may differ from GPT-4o at experiment time. The `raw_model` field in `.meta.json` records what was actually served.
- **Plainly density metric:** The `density` score counts specific pattern matches; it can be gamed by an intervention that removes those exact patterns without actually making the text read as more human.
- **Deslop circular dependency:** The fixer model (claude-sonnet-4.6) is also an LLM, and its edits may introduce new LLM tells even while removing the targeted ones.
- **Judge call count:** 3 interventions × 10 available models × 10 prompts = 300 judge calls (a few fewer where an intervention generation was missing). Manageable but still a limited sample.
- **Missing models:** Grok 4.3 and Mistral Large could not be reached on this account, so the frontier and mid tiers are thinner than designed (frontier = GPT-5.5 + Opus 4.7 only).
